issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .social-links a min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links are 28px tall, below the 44px recommendation. Users with limited dexterity may have difficulty activating social links in the footer.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility sets position: static and clip: auto, making content visible on screen. Screen reader users will hear duplicated content, while sighted users will see extraneous text, causing confusion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has transform animations on hover without a reduced-motion override. Users with vestibular disorders may experience dizziness or nausea when these animations trigger unexpectedly.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 570: transform: none; box-shadow: var(--shadow-xl);

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: Body text has word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses words together. Users with dyslexia, low vision, or cognitive disabilities may find text harder to read and distinguish between words.
issuesFound.fix: Line 60: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #555555 / background #ffffff ratio 4.0:1
issuesFound.explanation: The primary text colour (#555555) on white background has a contrast ratio of approximately 4.0:1, below the 4.5:1 minimum. Users with low vision or colour deficiencies will struggle to read body text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 17: --colour-neutral-900: #444444;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #5588bb / background #ffffff ratio 2.9:1
issuesFound.explanation: The brand primary colour (#5588bb) on white background has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1, below the 4.5:1 minimum. Users with low vision cannot read text in this colour, such as button labels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3: --colour-brand-primary: #2266aa;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: All links have text-decoration: none with no alternative visual differentiator. Users with colour blindness or low vision cannot distinguish links from surrounding text, breaking navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 70: text-decoration: underline;

issuesFound.title: Font size below 16px on body text
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, reducing the base font size below 16px. Users with low vision or reading disabilities may find text too small to read comfortably.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on :focus-visible without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator, such as box-shadow or border. This removes focus visibility for keyboard users, making navigation difficult or impossible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 200: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Body text font-size below 16px readability concern
issuesFound.explanation: The root font-size is set to 12px, making body text effectively 12px via rem units, which is below the 16px recommended for readability. This can impair reading for users with visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 50: html { font-size: 16px; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words, making text harder to read, especially for users with dyslexia or visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 60: body { word-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing: -0.05em, which reduces space between letters, potentially decreasing text legibility for users with visual or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 60: body { letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility does not hide content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which does not visually hide content while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This may cause visible clutter or incorrect announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 180: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-image img has transform animations that cause spatial movement, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for it is empty, lacking an override to disable or slow motion for users with motion sensitivities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 360: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: #search-input has an explicit height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 280: #search-input { height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-4); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-sm has a min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size, affecting touch accessibility for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 240: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-4); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-icon has min-width and min-height of 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size, reducing accuracy for touch interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 245: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Without an alternative non-color visual differentiator like font-weight or border, links may be hard to distinguish for color-blind users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 90: a { text-decoration: underline; } or add context-specific underlines on hover, e.g., a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px on icon button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px. With padding, the total touch target is 40x40px, which is below the recommended 44x44px minimum for touch targets. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the button.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px on small button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height to 20px. With padding, the total height is 32px, which is below the recommended 44px minimum touch target height.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input has a height of 28px, which is below the 44px minimum touch target height. This can make it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 680: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has width and height of 28px with no padding, resulting in a 28x28px touch target, below the 44x44px minimum.
issuesFound.fix: Line 700: width: 44px; height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility does not hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility sets properties that make content visible (position: static, clip: auto, etc.). This fails to hide content visually while keeping it available to screen readers, breaking accessibility for users who rely on screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Replace the .sr-only rule with a correct implementation: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transform transition on hover, but the reduced motion media query does not disable it. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience unnecessary movement.
issuesFound.fix: In the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 800, add: .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover has a transform animation, but there is no reduced motion override for this specific selector. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience unexpected movement.
issuesFound.fix: In the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 1100, add: .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can make text more difficult to read, especially for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 80: Remove word-spacing: -0.2em; or set it to a non-negative value.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for brand primary text on white
issuesFound.explanation: The colour --colour-brand-primary (#5588bb) used as text colour on --colour-text-on-brand (white) has a contrast ratio of 3.5:1, which is below the minimum 4.5:1 for normal text. Users with low vision may not be able to read the text.
issuesFound.fix: Change the value of --colour-brand-primary to a darker shade, e.g., #004aaa (which has a contrast ratio of 7.0:1 on white).

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The base a rule removes underlines from all links, relying solely on colour to distinguish links from surrounding text. Users with colour blindness or low vision may not be able to identify links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 120: a { text-decoration: underline; } or provide an alternative visual cue such as a border-bottom or background color change.

fpTitles: Touch target may be below 44px on small button
Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for featured pricing card

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open function changes visible state but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users won't know the menu state changed.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav.open function, add: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav close function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation close function changes visible state but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users won't know the menu state changed.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav.close function, add: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown open function shows the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users won't know the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix: In the _openInstance function, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: Dropdown close function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown close function hides the panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users won't know the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix: In the _closeInstance function, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle switch changes state but does not update aria-pressed on the toggle element. Screen reader users won't know the toggle's current state.
issuesFound.fix: In the _onToggleClick function, add: setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, next === 'year');

issuesFound.title: Product filter dynamic update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count changes dynamically but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users won't know the results have updated.
issuesFound.fix: In the applyFilters function, after _updateCountBadge, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied?.(s.activeCategory) || `Filter applied, showing ${totalMatches} products`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is invalid, the code adds a CSS class but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers won't announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: In the _renderResult function, in the else block (invalid), add: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true); In the valid block, add: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on mobile nav trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded set initially. Screen reader users won't know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav.init function, after _trigger is assigned, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on dropdown triggers
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded="false". Screen reader users won't know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In the register function, after panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''), add: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

fpTitles: aria-expanded not initialised on dropdown triggers

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on mobile navigation trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open, mobileNav.close, and mobileNav.toggle functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the expanded/collapsed state of the navigation menu.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on dropdown menu trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions show/hide dropdown panels but do not set aria-expanded on the associated trigger buttons. Screen reader users will not know whether the dropdown is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2164: function _openInstance(inst) { ... setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); ... }
Line 2178: function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { ... setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); ... }

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed for billing period selection
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function updates visual active states for billing period buttons but does not set aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot perceive which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3334: function _syncToggleState(period) { ... _periodBtns.forEach(btn => { ... setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive); }); ... }

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers for product filtering
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible product grid but does not announce the change via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not informed when filtered results are displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2900: function applyFilters() { ... liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : `Filter applied, showing ${_productFilterState.activeCategory} products`); ... }

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers for price changes
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates displayed prices but does not announce the billing period change via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not informed when prices switch between monthly and annual billing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3318: function setPeriod(period, animate) { ... liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly); ... }

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load for dropdown menu triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.init function does not set initial aria-expanded="false" on dropdown trigger buttons. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state of dropdown menus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250: function init() { ... _instances.forEach(inst => setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false)); ... }

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load for mobile navigation trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function does not set initial aria-expanded="false" on the mobile menu toggle button. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state of the mobile navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2122: function init() { ... setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); ... }

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open, close, and toggle functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's state, breaking WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2030: In the open function, after `_open = true;`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. Line 2050: In the close function, after `_open = false;`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`. Line 2070: Ensure the toggle function calls open/close which now update the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle switch does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle (month/year) in the pricing module changes state visually but does not set aria-pressed on the switch element. Screen reader users cannot perceive the current toggle state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In the _syncToggleState function, after toggling the class, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', isYear);` where isYear is true for 'year' period.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible product grid and count but does not announce the change via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users may not know the content has updated, degrading the experience (WCAG 4.1.3).
issuesFound.fix: In the callback of filterAnimation.run, add a live region announcement, e.g., `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function marks fields invalid with CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not announce the invalid state, breaking WCAG 4.1.2 for form error indication.
issuesFound.fix: In the _renderResult function, in the else branch (invalid), add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. In the valid branch, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger does not have aria-expanded initialized on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function does not set an initial aria-expanded value on the navigation trigger button. Screen readers may report an incorrect or missing state when the page loads, causing confusion (WCAG 4.1.2).
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav.init function, after the trigger is assigned, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` (assuming the menu is closed by default).

issuesFound.title: Missing lang attribute on html element
issuesFound.explanation: The document does not specify a language attribute. Screen readers and other assistive technologies rely on this to announce content in the correct language and pronunciation. Users who rely on screen readers will have a degraded experience.
issuesFound.fix: Add lang="en" to the <html> element in the root document (fix requires changes in external HTML file).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions add and remove CSS classes to show/hide the navigation drawer, but they never set or clear the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Without this attribute screen‑reader users cannot know whether the navigation menu is currently expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
```js
function open(announceToSR) {
  …
  setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');   // <-- add
  …
}
function close(announceToSR) {
  …
  setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); // <-- add
  …
}
```  
Also initialise the button with `aria-expanded="false"` on page load.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator._renderResult()` calls `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl)` which only adds `aria-describedby`. It never sets the `aria‑invalid` attribute, so screen‑reader users are not informed that a field is in an error state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers lack an initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: `faqRegistry.parse()` creates each FAQ item’s trigger button, adds `aria-controls` and `aria-labelledby`, but does not set `aria‑expanded`. When the accordion loads, the expanded/collapsed state of each trigger is undefined for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
After determining `isInitiallyOpen`, set the attribute accordingly:  
```js
const isOpen = isInitiallyOpen;
setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', isOpen ? 'true' : 'false'); // <-- add
```
Ensure the default state is `false` for collapsed items.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter.applyFilters()` updates the visual count badge (`_updateCountBadge`) but does not provide an ARIA live announcement. Users of screen readers receive no feedback that the number of matching products has changed after filtering.

issuesFound.title: Search combobox missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is turned into a combobox (role="combobox") with aria‑autocomplete and aria‑controls, but no aria‑expanded attribute is set or updated to reflect the visibility of the suggestion list. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the list is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when the component is created and toggle it to “true”/“false” in `_open()` / `_close()` of the site‑search controller.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that open/close dropdown panels are given aria‑controls, but the code never sets or toggles aria‑expanded on the trigger element when the panel is shown or hidden. This leaves the state ambiguous for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance(inst)` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `_closeInstance(inst)` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons are given role="button" and aria‑controls, but the script never updates aria‑expanded when an item is opened or closed. Users of assistive technology cannot perceive the open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqAccordion.open(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `faqAccordion.close(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing toggle (`SELECTORS.billingToggle`) visually indicates annual vs. monthly pricing, but the button does not expose its state via aria‑pressed, making the current period invisible to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: In `_syncToggleState(period)` add `setAttr(_toggleBtn, 'aria-pressed', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');` and ensure the attribute is set on init.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on error fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator.validate()` routine marks fields as invalid by adding error text, but it never adds `aria-invalid="true"` (or removes it on success). Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator.validate()` after a failed validation call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` and on success call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`. Also update `markInvalid`/`markValid` helpers accordingly.

issuesFound.fix: Line 462: <label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">

issuesFound.fix: Line 1085: `<button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>`

issuesFound.title: Filter tab “Scanning” button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.fix: Line 1089: add `aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}` to the button (same pattern as Issue 9).

issuesFound.title: Filter tab “Design” button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.fix: Line 1093: add `aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}`.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab “Testing” button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.fix: Line 1097: add `aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}`.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab “Reporting” button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.fix: Line 1101: add `aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}`.

issuesFound.title: Theme toggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: `<IconButton>` used for the theme toggle contains only an icon (☀️/🌙/💻) and does not provide an `aria-label`, making it invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 950: `<IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle" aria-label={LABELS[theme]}>`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The close button in the mobile navigation (`<IconButton onClick={onClose}>✕</IconButton>`) has no `aria-label`, so its purpose is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540: `<IconButton onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close" aria-label="Close navigation menu">✕</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel previous control IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: `<IconButton aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev}>‹</IconButton>` provides only a visual arrow without an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1245: `<IconButton aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} aria-label="Previous testimonial">‹</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel next control IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: `<IconButton aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext}>›</IconButton>` lacks an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1252: `<IconButton aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} aria-label="Next testimonial">›</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation `<nav id="main-nav">` missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The primary navigation landmark has no `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`, making it hard for screen reader users to distinguish it from other navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 306: `<nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Primary navigation" className="desktop-nav">`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation `<nav className="mobile-nav__body">` missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation region lacks an `aria-label`/`aria-labelledby`, so its purpose is unclear among multiple `<nav>` landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 558: `<nav className="mobile-nav__body" aria-label="Mobile primary navigation">`

issuesFound.title: Skip‑links navigation `<nav>` missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The skip‑link container is a `<nav>` element without an `aria-label`, which can be confusing for AT users navigating landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 236: `<nav aria-label="Skip links">`

issuesFound.title: Hero section missing `aria-labelledby`
issuesFound.explanation: `<section className="hero-section">` does not reference its heading (`h1 id="hero-heading"`), so screen readers cannot associate the region with a name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1159: `<section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">`

issuesFound.title: Stats bar section missing `aria-labelledby`
issuesFound.explanation: `<section className="stats-bar">` lacks an accessible name; it should reference the hidden `<h2 id="stats-heading">`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240: `<section className="stats-bar" aria-labelledby="stats-heading">`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label elements lack `htmlFor` association (multiple instances)
issuesFound.explanation:
The `<label className="form-label">` inside `FormGroup` does not use `htmlFor`, so inputs are not programmatically associated with their labels. This occurs in the newsletter form and each field of the contact form.  
  Fix (example for line 1385): `Line 1385: <label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">` (apply the same change to lines 1402, 1419, 1436, 1453).

issuesFound.title: TextInput components do not set `aria-invalid` when `invalid` prop is true (multiple instances)
issuesFound.explanation:
The custom `TextInput` component forwards the `invalid` prop but never adds `aria-invalid="true"`, leaving error state undisclosed.  
  Fix (example for line 1399): `Line 1399: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={...} {...rest} />` (apply to all listed lines).

issuesFound.title: Required‑field visual asterisk not hidden from AT (multiple instances)
issuesFound.explanation: The ` *` is announced by screen readers, causing confusion. It should have `aria-hidden="true"`. Fix (example for line 1387): `Line 1387: *` (apply to all listed lines).

issuesFound.title: Submit button in newsletter form missing `aria-busy` during loading
issuesFound.explanation: The `<Button>` used for form submission receives a `loading` prop (which is ignored) and does not convey its busy state via `aria-busy`, so AT cannot know the button is processing.
issuesFound.fix: Replace `loading={isSubmitting}` with `isLoading={isSubmitting}` and ensure the `Button` component adds `aria-busy={isLoading}` (modify the component or pass `aria-busy` directly). Example fix for line 1369: `Line 1369: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-busy={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}>`

issuesFound.title: Submit button in contact form missing `aria-busy` during loading
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 26; the button receives `loading={isSubmitting}` which is ignored and lacks `aria-busy`.
issuesFound.fix: Change to `isLoading={isSubmitting}` and add `aria-busy={isSubmitting}`. Example: `Line 1515: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-busy={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting}>`

issuesFound.title: Spinner element inside loading button missing `aria-hidden` (newsletter)
issuesFound.explanation: When `isLoading` is true, the `<span className="btn-spinner" />` is not hidden from AT, causing unnecessary noise.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1365: `<span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />`

issuesFound.title: Spinner element inside loading button missing `aria-hidden` (contact)
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 28 for the contact form submit button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1511: `<span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />`

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link in desktop menu missing `aria-current`
issuesFound.explanation: The link for the current page (`className="nav-link nav-link--current"`) does not have `aria-current="page"`, so AT cannot identify the active page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 322: `<a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link in mobile menu missing `aria-current`
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation link for the current page lacks `aria-current="page"`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 585: `<a href={item.href} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} onClick={onClose}>`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email field `aria-describedby` points to non‑existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The `TextInput` includes `aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined}` but no element with id `newsletter-email-error` is rendered, breaking the reference.
issuesFound.fix: Add an error element with that id (e.g., `<ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" message={email.state.error} />`) or remove the `aria-describedby` when no error element exists.

issuesFound.title: Contact form field “Full name” `aria-describedby` points to missing element
issuesFound.explanation: References `contact-name-error` which is never rendered.
issuesFound.fix: Render `<ErrorMessage id="contact-name-error" message={name.state.error} />` inside the form group or remove the reference.

issuesFound.title: Contact form field “Email address” `aria-describedby` points to missing element
issuesFound.fix: Render `<ErrorMessage id="contact-email-error" message={email.state.error} />` or remove the reference.

issuesFound.title: Contact form field “Subject” `aria-describedby` points to missing element
issuesFound.fix: Render `<ErrorMessage id="contact-subject-error" message={subject.state.error} />` or remove the reference.

issuesFound.title: Contact form field “Message” `aria-describedby` includes missing error id
issuesFound.explanation: References `contact-message-error` which does not exist.
issuesFound.fix: Render `<ErrorMessage id="contact-message-error" message={message.state.error} />` or remove the reference.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-sm` rule sets `min-height: 20px`, which is below the 24×24 px minimum touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). Touch and pointer users may struggle to activate small buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-icon` rule sets `min-width: 20px` and `min-height: 20px`, which is below the 24×24 px minimum touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). Touch and pointer users may struggle to activate icon-only buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1160: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The `.nav-toggle` rule sets `min-height: 28px` and `min-width: 28px`, which is below the 44×44 px recommended touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8 AAA). Touch users may struggle to activate the mobile nav toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1220: `.nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: var(--space-2); background: none; border: 1px solid var(--colour-border); border-radius: var(--radius-md); color: var(--colour-text-primary); font-size: var(--font-size-xl); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button
issuesFound.explanation: The rule sets `min-height: 28px`, which is below the 44×44 px recommended touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8 AAA). Touch users may struggle to activate nav links and dropdown buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1235: `#nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: The rule sets `min-height: 28px`, which is below the 44×44 px recommended touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8 AAA). Touch users may struggle to activate dropdown menu items.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1255: `[id$="-submenu"] li a { min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: The `.filter-tabs button` rule sets `min-height: 28px`, which is below the 44×44 px recommended touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8 AAA). Touch users may struggle to activate filter tabs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1730: `.filter-tabs button { min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The `.pricing-toggle button` rule sets `min-height: 28px`, which is below the 44×44 px recommended touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8 AAA). Touch users may struggle to activate pricing toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1840: `.pricing-toggle button { min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .faq-item dt button
issuesFound.explanation: The `.faq-item dt button` rule sets `min-height: 60px`, which is sufficient for height but the button's width is not constrained. On small screens, touch users may struggle to activate the full width. Ensure the button spans the full container width.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2140: `.faq-item dt button { width: 100%; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img` and `.hero-image img:hover` rules use `transform` with perspective and rotation, creating motion animation. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or slow this animation, failing WCAG 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The `.feature-card:hover` rule uses `transform: translateY(-2px)`, creating motion animation. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or slow this animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The `.product-card:hover` rule uses `transform: translateY(-3px)`, creating motion animation. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or slow this animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The `.pricing-card:hover` rule uses `transform: translateY(-2px)`, creating motion animation. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or slow this animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The `.pricing-card--featured:hover` rule uses `transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01)`, creating motion animation. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or slow this animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .stat-item:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The `.stat-item:hover` rule uses `transform: translateY(-2px)`, creating motion animation. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or slow this animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .faq-item dt button::after
issuesFound.explanation: The `.faq-item dt button::after` rule uses `transform: rotate(90deg)` and `transition: transform`, creating motion animation. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or slow this animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card a:hover::after
issuesFound.explanation: The `.feature-card a:hover::after` rule uses `transform: translateX(3px)`, creating motion animation. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or slow this animation.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The `a { text-decoration: none; }` rule removes underlines from all links, relying solely on colour to distinguish them from surrounding text. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color) as colour alone is not sufficient for users with low vision or colour blindness.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: Remove `text-decoration: none;` from the `a` selector and apply it only to specific link types (e.g., buttons, nav links) where underlines are undesirable. For body text links, retain underlines or add a non-colour visual indicator (e.g., border-bottom, font-weight increase on hover/focus).

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` rule sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision by compressing word spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 180: Remove `word-spacing: -0.2em;` from the `body` rule.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` rule sets `letter-spacing: -0.05em`, which can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision by compressing letter spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 180: Remove `letter-spacing: -0.05em;` from the `body` rule.

issuesFound.title: Font size below 16px on html element
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` rule sets `font-size: 12px`, which is below the 16px (1rem) minimum recommended for readability (WCAG 1.4.4 Resize Text). Users with low vision may struggle to read text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 250: `html { font-size: 16px; }`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for non-native interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav._onTriggerClick` function (lines 1120–1130) handles click events on the mobile navigation toggle button but does not include a corresponding keyboard event handler for Enter and Space keys. This means keyboard users cannot activate the mobile menu, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).
issuesFound.fix: Add a keydown event handler to the trigger button that listens for Enter (key 'Enter') and Space (key ' ') keys and calls the same toggle logic as the click handler.

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for non-native interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button (`_onTriggerClick`) is bound to click events but lacks matching keydown handlers for Enter and Space. This prevents keyboard users from activating the mobile menu, violating WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in `mobileNav` toggles the mobile menu state but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). This violates WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value, preventing screen readers from announcing the menu's open/closed state.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function in `pricing` updates the displayed price and savings message but does not announce the change to screen readers via a live region. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages, preventing users from being informed of the billing period change.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` function in `inlineValidator` identifies invalid fields but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field element. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages, preventing screen readers from announcing the error state.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` function in `inlineValidator` clears the error message on valid input but does not remove or set `aria-invalid="false"` on the field element. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages, leaving the field in an inconsistent state.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `mobileNav` does not initialise the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`) to reflect the initial closed state of the mobile menu. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value, leaving the button in an ambiguous state.

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute on personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form email input (`_emailEl`) lacks an `autocomplete` attribute. This violates WCAG 1.3.5 Identify Input Purpose, preventing browsers and assistive technologies from auto-filling the email address.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button (`_trigger`) lacks an accessible name. The button uses an icon (hamburger) but has no `aria-label`, `aria-labelledby`, or text content, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference in aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The `register` function in `dropdownMenu` sets `aria-controls` on the trigger button to point to the panel's id (`panelId`), but the panel element may not have an id if it was not set in the HTML. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value, creating a broken reference.

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference in aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The `parse` function in `faqRegistry` sets `aria-labelledby` on the panel element to point to the trigger's id (`triggerId`), but the trigger element may not have an id if it was not set in the HTML. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value, creating a broken reference.

issuesFound.title: Missing role on interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button (`_trigger`) may not be a native `<button>` element. If it is a `<div>` or `<span>`, it lacks a `role="button"` attribute, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.

issuesFound.title: Missing focusable state on interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button (`_trigger`) may not be keyboard-focusable. If it is not a native `<button>` and lacks a `tabindex="0"` attribute, it violates WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle element (`.nav-toggle`) is enhanced with pointer events but lacks matching keyboard handlers for Enter/Space. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot activate the mobile menu.

issuesFound.title: Decorative icon missing aria-hidden={true}
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon inside the NavDropdown trigger button is decorative but not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers will announce the arrow characters, which is redundant since the button's aria-expanded state already conveys the open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1502: `<span aria-hidden="true" className="nav-link__chevron">`

issuesFound.title: Required-field marker announces bare asterisk to AT — add aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The required-field marker (asterisk) is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers will announce "asterisk" which is not meaningful without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 850: ` *`

issuesFound.title: Toggle component trigger missing aria-expanded prop

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the default focus ring for all keyboard-focusable elements, making it impossible for keyboard-only users to track focus position.

issuesFound.title: Forced-colors mode focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block removes the outline for :focus-visible without providing a high-contrast alternative such as a solid border or outline using system colors. Users in forced-colors mode lose all focus indication.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which renders the content visible to sighted users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies. This defeats the purpose of the utility.

issuesFound.title: Base font size below 16px impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, which is below the recommended 16px minimum for body text. This causes all rem-based text to be smaller than expected, reducing readability for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing reduces text readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses the space between words. This makes text harder to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities who rely on clear word boundaries.

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line height below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The p selector sets line-height: 1, which is below the recommended 1.5 for body text. This tight vertical spacing reduces readability for users with low vision or reading disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator such as box-shadow or border-color change. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Small button variant touch target below 24px minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this button.

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below 24px minimum

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on table not-included cells
issuesFound.explanation: The `.comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not included"]` selector uses `color: var(--colour-neutral-300)` (`#cbd5e1`) against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.47:1, which is far below the required 4.5:1 for text content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1350: .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not included"], .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not available"] { color: var(--colour-neutral-500); font-size: var(--font-size-xl); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a` selector inherits `text-decoration: none` from the global `a` rule and only provides a colour change (`color: var(--colour-brand-primary)`) on hover, failing to provide a non-colour visual differentiator for users who cannot perceive colour differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 810: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a` selector inherits `text-decoration: none` and only changes colour on hover, relying solely on colour to distinguish the link state, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color for users with colour vision deficiencies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1525: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The `.social-links a` selector inherits `text-decoration: none` and only provides a colour change on hover, failing to provide a non-colour visual indicator that the element is a link for users who cannot perceive colour.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1550: .social-links a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }

Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero images

Insufficient colour contrast on section header paragraphs
Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter input hints
Insufficient colour contrast on table not-included cells
Link underline removed without alternative on footer navigation

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus management on open and close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not moved into the drawer, leaving keyboard users with focus on the trigger button while the visual context changes. When the drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button, causing focus to be lost to the document body.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1938: In the open function, after `_lockScroll();`, add `_drawer.focus({ preventScroll: true });` (ensuring the drawer has tabindex="-1" if not focusable by default) or focus the first focusable element inside the drawer using `getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0].focus();`.
  Line 1955: In the close function, before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', ...);`, add `_trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true });` to return focus to the trigger.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox (role="combobox") controlling a suggestion listbox. The _open and _close functions toggle the visibility of the suggestion panel but do not update aria-expanded on the input element. The init function also fails to initialise aria-expanded to "false". Screen readers cannot announce whether the suggestion list is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2620: In _open, add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_searchState.open = true;`.
  Line 2628: In _close, add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_searchState.open = false;`.
  Line 2732: In init, after setting attributes on _inputEl, add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded attribute updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels via _openInstance and _closeInstance, but neither function sets aria-expanded on the trigger button to reflect the panel's visibility state. The register function also fails to initialise aria-expanded to "false" when registering a new dropdown instance.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2067: In _openInstance, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`.
  Line 2078: In _closeInstance, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`.
  Line 2143: In register, after setting aria-controls, add `if (!trigger.getAttribute(ARIA.EXPANDED)) setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger missing aria-expanded in open/close functions
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open() and close() functions animate the panel height and update CSS classes, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect whether the answer panel is expanded or collapsed. Although init() sets the initial state, subsequent toggles are not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3323: In open, add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `item.open = true;`.
  Line 3343: In close, add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button missing aria-pressed attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button acts as a toggle for the carousel's automatic rotation. The play() and pause() functions update the aria-label to reflect the action that will happen next, but they do not set aria-pressed to indicate the current state of the toggle (true when playing, false when paused). This makes it impossible for screen reader users to determine if the carousel is currently auto-playing or paused.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3472: In play, add `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, true);` after `_playing = true;`.
  Line 3484: In pause, add `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, false);` after `_playing = false;`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period selection buttons (monthly/quarterly/yearly) function as toggle buttons where one is always selected. The _syncToggleState function updates CSS classes to show the active state, but it does not set aria-pressed="true" on the active button or aria-pressed="false" on inactive buttons. Screen readers cannot identify which period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2973: In _syncToggleState, inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off toggle switch (month vs year) has role="switch" but does not set aria-checked to reflect whether the switch is in the "on" (year) or "off" (month) state. The _syncToggleState function only toggles CSS classes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2980: In _syncToggleState, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, isYear);` after `toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear);`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-pressed attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter tabs act as toggle buttons where the active tab is visually indicated via CSS classes. The _syncTabAriaStates function updates the active class but does not set aria-pressed to indicate the pressed state of the buttons. Screen readers cannot identify which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3144: In _syncTabAriaStates, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as invalid, the markInvalid function adds the error message to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field element. Screen readers cannot identify that the field contains an invalid value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1566: In markInvalid, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearing
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as valid, the markValid function removes the error reference from aria-describedby but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false". The field remains marked as invalid to screen readers even after the error is corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1585: In markValid, add `field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);` after the if block that updates aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled (monthly/yearly), the prices update visually with animation, but the change is not announced to screen reader users. Users relying on screen readers cannot perceive that the prices have changed or what the new prices are.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2953: In setPeriod, after updating the plan cards, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` (or construct an appropriate message with the new price).

fpTitles: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-pressed attribute

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but the _open and _close functions never toggle aria-expanded to reflect the suggestion listbox visibility. Screen readers cannot announce whether suggestions are available or when the popup opens/closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1820 (in _open): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_searchState.open = true;`
  Line 1829 (in _close): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_searchState.open = false;`
  Line 1854 (in init): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When expanding a FAQ item, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true" (though close sets it to "false"). This leaves screen readers unaware that the panel is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2496 (in open function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not update its aria-expanded attribute when the drawer opens or closes, leaving assistive technologies unaware of the visibility state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1450 (in open): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;`
  Line 1467 (in close): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the updated product count is rendered visually but not announced to screen readers via a live region, leaving blind users unaware that the grid has updated or how many items match.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1966 (in applyFilters): After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(\`\${totalMatches} products found\`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates prices visually but does not announce the change to screen readers, so users may not be aware the prices have changed or which period is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2188 (in setPeriod): After `_syncToggleState(period);`, add `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices switched to \${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing\`);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function updates aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true", so screen readers do not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1184 (in markInvalid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the function body (e.g., after the isElement checks).

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation errors, the function removes the error from aria-describedby but leaves aria-invalid="true", causing screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1195 (in markValid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` inside the function body (e.g., after the refs join).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false", so screen readers do not know the drawer is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1485 (in init): After `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input is not initialised with aria-expanded="false", violating the required state for comboboxes with collapsed popups.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1854 (in init): After `setAttr(input, 'aria-controls', _listId);`, add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion non-button triggers lack keyboard activation
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ triggers that are not native button elements (assigned role="button") cannot be activated with Enter or Space keys because the keydown handler only manages arrow navigation, not activation.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open does not move focus to drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not moved from the trigger button into the drawer content, leaving keyboard users stranded at the trigger.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button, violating focus restoration requirements for modal dialogs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1467 (in close): Before the closing brace, add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm email missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input (type="email") lacks the autocomplete="email" attribute, preventing browsers from autofill and violating WCAG 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2744 (in init): After `_emailEl = qs('input[type="email"]', _formEl);`, add:
  `if (_emailEl && !_emailEl.hasAttribute('autocomplete')) setAttr(_emailEl, 'autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: contactForm personal fields missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form binds to name and email fields but does not ensure they have autocomplete attributes (full_name should have "name", email should have "email").
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2804 (in init): After binding validation for each field, add:
  For the name field: `if(field) setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'name');`
  For the email field: `if(field) setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'email');`

fpTitles: faqAccordion non-button triggers lack keyboard activation
mobileNav open does not move focus to drawer
newsletterForm email missing autocomplete
contactForm personal fields missing autocomplete

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing focus trap implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module declares a `_trap` variable for focus management but never initializes it with `createFocusTrap`. When the mobile menu opens, keyboard focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing users to tab to background elements behind the overlay, which blocks access for keyboard-only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2145: Initialize the trap: `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });`. Line 2160: Activate the trap: `_trap.activate();` inside the `open` function. Line 2185: Deactivate the trap: `_trap.deactivate();` inside the `close` function.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions in `dropdownMenu` show and hide the dropdown panels by removing or adding the `hidden` attribute, but they do not synchronize the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger buttons to reflect the visibility state of the panels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2420: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside `_openInstance`. Line 2440: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside `_closeInstance`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions in `faqAccordion` manage the visibility of answer panels and CSS classes, but fail to toggle the `aria-expanded` attribute on the question buttons (`item.trigger`). This leaves screen reader users unaware of whether an answer is expanded or collapsed after activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4820: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside the `open` function. Line 4845: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside the `close` function.

issuesFound.title: Search combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The site search combobox input (`_inputEl`) does not update its `aria-expanded` attribute when the suggestion listbox is opened via `_open()` or closed via `_close()`. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the visibility state of the suggestions dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3365: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside the `_open` function. Line 3375: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside the `_close` function.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period switch missing aria-checked state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle (`_toggleEl`) is assigned `role="switch"` but the `setPeriod()` function never updates the `aria-checked` attribute to reflect whether the switch is activated (e.g., annual billing) or not. Screen readers cannot convey the on/off state of the switch.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4430: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', period === 'year');` (or appropriate boolean based on the active period) inside the `setPeriod` function.

issuesFound.title: Form validation not setting aria-invalid on fields
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, `srUtils.markInvalid` only links the error message via `aria-describedby` but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. When validation passes, `markValid` removes the describedby reference but does not clear `aria-invalid`. This prevents screen readers from identifying invalid fields programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1980: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` inside `markInvalid`. Line 2000: Add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` (or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`) inside `markValid`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users apply category or sort filters, the `applyFilters` function updates the visible product cards and the count badge, but does not announce the change in results (e.g., "Showing 5 products") via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the content has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3945: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied?.(s.activeCategory) || `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);` after the filter animation completes.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the price values update visually with animation, but the change is not announced to screen reader users, leaving them unaware of the new prices or billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4435: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` to announce the billing period change.

issuesFound.title: Search combobox missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When initializing the search combobox, the input element is given `role="combobox"`, `aria-haspopup`, and `aria-controls`, but `aria-expanded="false"` is not set initially to indicate that the suggestion listbox is collapsed by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3305: Add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in the `mount` function after setting other ARIA attributes.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering dropdown triggers, the code sets `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls`, but does not initialize `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button while the panel is hidden by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2515: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after setting `aria-controls` in the `register` function.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: During initialization, the mobile navigation trigger button (`_trigger`) is selected and event listeners are attached, but `aria-expanded="false"` is not set initially to indicate that the drawer is closed by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2135: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in the `init` function after selecting the trigger element.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation toggle missing initial aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button uses aria-controls and aria-haspopup but lacks aria-expanded. Screen reader users cannot tell whether the submenu is currently open or closed, preventing them from understanding the disclosure state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1244: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent disclosure button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ trigger button controls a content panel via aria-controls but omits aria-expanded. Users of screen readers cannot perceive whether the answer panel is currently visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1784: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The close button for the mobile navigation panel is an IconButton containing only the "✕" symbol without an aria-label. Screen reader users hear only "button" with no indication of what it closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1294: <IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel previous-slide button is an IconButton with only the "‹" character and no aria-label. Screen reader users cannot identify that this navigates to the previous testimonial.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1859: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel next-slide button is an IconButton with only the "›" character and no aria-label. Screen reader users cannot identify that this navigates to the next testimonial.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1864: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email TextInput references aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error", but the FormGroup component is not passed errorId="newsletter-email-error", so the error message element lacks this ID. Screen readers cannot associate the error message with the input field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1930: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The name TextInput references aria-describedby="contact-name-error", but the FormGroup is not passed errorId="contact-name-error", so the ID does not exist in the DOM. Screen readers cannot announce the error message for this field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2016: <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email TextInput references aria-describedby="contact-email-error", but the FormGroup is not passed errorId="contact-email-error". The missing ID prevents screen readers from associating the error message with the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2032: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The subject TextInput references aria-describedby="contact-subject-error", but the FormGroup lacks errorId="contact-subject-error". Screen readers cannot locate the error message element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2048: <FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" error={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The message Textarea references aria-describedby including "contact-message-error", but the FormGroup is not passed errorId="contact-message-error". The error message is not programmatically linked to the textarea.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2065: <FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required errorId="contact-message-error" hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`} error={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker announces bare asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The required field indicator ( *) is not marked with aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce "star" after the label text, which is redundant with the visually hidden "(required)" text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 570: *

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star characters (★ and ☆) inside StarRating are not marked with aria-hidden. While the parent has an aria-label, some screen readers may still announce the literal star characters in addition to the label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 538-540: {filled ? '★' : '☆'}

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation link for the current page has a visual indicator class but no aria-current="page" attribute. Screen readers cannot announce the active page status.
