issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing toggle button lacks aria-pressed, preventing screen reader users from knowing that this button represents the selected billing period when active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1692: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter category buttons act as toggle buttons that remain pressed when selected, but they lack aria-pressed. Assistive technology users cannot perceive which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1561-1574: <button type="button" aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a <nav> element without an aria-label. Since multiple navigation landmarks exist on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish this skip navigation from other navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 554: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation uses a <nav> element without an aria-label. With multiple navigation regions present, users cannot differentiate the main navigation from footer or skip navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav internal navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation element inside the mobile dialog lacks an aria-label. This creates an unnamed navigation landmark within the dialog, confusing screen reader landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1304: <nav aria-label="Mobile" className="mobile-nav__body">

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: When the Button component is in a loading state (isLoading=true), it renders a spinner but does not set aria-busy="true" on the button element. Screen reader users are not notified that the button is processing an action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 596: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The modal dialog container has role="dialog" but lacks aria-modal="true", and the aria-modal attribute is incorrectly placed on the overlay div. Screen reader users may navigate outside the modal while it is open, and the modal nature is not communicated.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2980: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
  Fix: Line 2983: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav dialog missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel uses role="dialog" and aria-label but omits aria-modal="true". Without this attribute, screen reader users may not perceive the dialog as modal and could navigate outside of it while it is open.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true" on field
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function links a field to its error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the input field. Screen reader users are not notified that the field contains an error, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1910-1915: function markInvalid(field, errorEl) { if (!isElement(field) || !isElement(errorEl)) return; setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() function removes the error description reference but never clears aria-invalid from the field. The field remains marked as invalid even after the error is resolved, providing incorrect state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1925-1940: function markValid(field, errorEl) { if (!isElement(field)) return; field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); if (isElement(errorEl) && errorEl.id) { const refs = (field.getAttribute('aria-describedby') || '').split(/\s+/).filter(id => id && id !== errorEl.id); if (refs.length) { field.setAttribute('aria-describedby', refs.join(' ')); } else { field.removeAttribute('aria-describedby'); } } }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function sets up the navigation toggle but never initializes aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. The initial state is not communicated to screen readers, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2490-2520: function init() { if (_initialised) return; _trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle); _drawer = qs(SELECTORS.navDrawer || SELECTORS.primaryNav); _overlay = qs(SELECTORS.navOverlay); if (!_trigger || !_drawer) { debug('mobileNav.init: trigger or drawer not found — skipping'); return; } _initialised = true; setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); _trigger.addEventListener('click', _onTriggerClick);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.init() does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.init() function registers dropdown triggers but never initializes aria-expanded="false" on them. The initial collapsed state is not communicated to screen readers, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2620-2650: function init() { if (_initialised) return; _initialised = true; qsa('[data-dropdown]').forEach(wrapper => { const trigger = qs('button[aria-expanded], button[aria-haspopup]', wrapper) || qs('button', wrapper); const panel = qs('[role="menu"], [role="listbox"], ul, [data-dropdown-panel]', wrapper); if (trigger && panel) { setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); register(trigger, panel); } });

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never created or activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares _trap = null but never calls createFocusTrap() to create a focus trap, nor activates it when the drawer opens. Keyboard users can Tab outside the modal drawer, violating WCAG 2.4.11 (Focus Not Obscured).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2885: _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { escapeDeactivates: false }); _trap.activate();

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen reader users are not notified that the field contains invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4750: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error description but never clears aria-invalid from the field. Screen reader users may continue to hear that the field is invalid after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4765: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() does not initialise aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button does not have aria-expanded initialised to "false" on page load. Screen readers may not announce the correct state until the button is first clicked.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2950: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialise aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown trigger, aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" even though the panel starts hidden. Screen readers may not announce the correct initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3300: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function sets item.open to true and adds CSS classes, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2831: add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function in srUtils adds aria-describedby to link the field to its error message, but never sets aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not announce that the field has an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5150: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the aria-describedby reference but does not clear aria-invalid. Fields that were previously invalid remain marked as invalid even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5163: add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after the aria-describedby handling block

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle not initialised with aria-expanded="false"
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function sets up event listeners but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger element. The initial state is undefined, which may cause screen readers to misreport the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2688: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: Product filter count changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge() function updates the visible count text but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when filtering reduces or expands the visible product count.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3781: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(count) || \`Showing \${count} products\`);` after `setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-label', \`\${count} product\${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''}\`);`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod() function updates prices and emits an event but does not announce the billing period change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when switching between monthly and annual pricing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3533: add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? (STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing') : (STRINGS.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'));` after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal placed on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div, but should be on the element with role="dialog". According to ARIA specifications, aria-modal must be on the same element as role="dialog" to properly communicate the modal state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1830: Remove aria-modal="true" from modal-overlay div. Line ~1835: Add aria-modal="true" to the dialog div (alongside role="dialog").

issuesFound.title: MobileNav panel missing aria-modal attribute
